web.title: Rōmaji to Hiragana Converter - PracticeJapanese.nl
web.description: 1. Enter any text into the "Enter Rōmaji" box. 2. The tool will try to convert (where possible) the text to Hiragana.
web.markdown:
![logo](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/img/pj_logo.png)

# Rōmaji to Hiragana Converter

# ろおまじ > ひらがな・コンバーター

- [Home](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/index.html)
- [Hiragana & Katakana](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/practice_hiragana_katakana.html "Practice Hiragana & Katakana")
- [Japanese Numbers](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/practice_japanese_numbers.html "Practice Japanese Numbers")
- [Calendar (Alpha)](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/practice_japanese_dates_times.html "Practice Japanese calendar related words & phrases.")
- [Hiragana Converter](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/romaji_into_hiragana_converter.html "Rōmaji into Hiragana Converter")
- [Katakana Converter](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/katakana_converter.html)
- [Info & Contact](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/contact.php)

![reverse order](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/img/reverse.png)![back](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/img/back2.png)

Introduction:

Hiragana Converter. V1.0.9

With this tool you can convert Rōmaji to Hiragana.

This tool is meant to quickly convert some text, for a more perminent solution of typing Japanese see the "Setting up Japanese for \[your system\]" guides on this page of the [Tae Kim's Guide To Japanese site](http://www.guidetojapanese.org/learn/resources/ "Link to page").

To learn Hiragana I would like to advice [this](https://www.tofugu.com/japanese/learn-hiragana/ "Link to page") guide from TOFUGO.

Disclaimer:

Everything stated / used here should be correct.
I am however just starting to learn myself and am not a expert in Japanese at all, so if you see any issues, errors and / or discrepancies please contact me at [contact@practicejapanese.nl](mailto:contact@practicejapanese.nl).

Instructions:

1\. Enter any text into the "Enter Rōmaji" box.


2\. The tool will try to convert (where possible) the text to Hiragana.


3\. The converted text can be found in the "Hiragana result" box.


4\. Any unconvertible characters / sounds will remain as is.

5\. A double nn followed by a space will become a single ん.


6a. Some "Double consonants" (pp, kk, tt, ss & tc) will automatically create a sokuon / small tsu (っ).


6b. Example: kk will become っk and issho will become いっしょ .


6c. This can be escaped by typing a \ before the double consonants (i\\ssho becomes いsしょ) .


6d. The small tsu (っ) can also be created by manually entering "ltsu" (for lowercase tsu).


7a. A normal dot ( . ) will be translated to a 。.


7b. A normal dot can be created by typing \\. (backslash dot).


8a. A くろまる ( ・ ) dot can be created by typing a / (forward slash).

8b. A normal / backslash can be created by typing \\/ (backslash + forward slash).

9a. A - will be translated to a ー.

9b. And the same as with the others this can be escaped with an \ (\\- becomes -).

10a. A double backslash ( \\\ ) will become a single slash and ignore the \\., sokuon and other escape rules.


10b. Example: \\\. will become \\。 and \\\kka will become \\っか.


11a. Single spaces will be removed, to create a single space two spaces need to be entered.


11b. Example: "juu ni" will become じゅうに, but "juu  ni" will become じゅう に.

![Expand](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/img/more_same_page.png)

Expand to show full translation dictionary...Translation dictionary (loose):

|     |     |     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- | --- | --- |
| あ = a | い = i | う = u | え = e | お = o |
| か = ko / co | き = ki / ci | く = ku / cu | け = ke / ce | こ = ko / co |
| が = ga | ぎ = gi | ぐ = gu | げ = ge | ご = go |
| さ = sa | し = shi / si | す = su | せ = se | そ = so |
| ざ = za | じ = ji | ず = zu | ぜ = ze | ぞ = zo |
| た = ta | ち = chi / ti | つ = tsu / tu | て = te | と = to |
| だ = da | ぢ = di / zi / dzi | づ = du / dzu | で = de | ど = do |
| な = na | に = ni | ぬ = nu | ね = ne | の = no |
| ば = ba | び = bi | ぶ = bu | べ = be | ぼ = bo |
| ぱ = pa | ぴ = pi | ぷ = pu | ぺ = pe | ぽ = po |
| ま = ma | み = mi | む = mu | め = me | も = mo |
| や = ya | ゆ = yu | よ = yo |  |  |
| ら = ra / la | り = ri / li | る = ru / lu | れ = re / le | ろ = ro / lo |
| わ = wa | ゐ = wi | ゑ = we | を = wo | ろ = ro / lo |
| ん = n / nn |  |  |  |  |

|     |     |     |
| --- | --- | --- |
| きゃ = kya / kja | きゅ = kyu / kju | きょ = kyo / kjo |
| ぎゃ = gya / gja | ぎゅ = gyu / gju | ぎょ = gyo / gju |
| しゃ = sha | しゅ = shu | しょ = sho |
| じゃ = jya / ja | じゅ = jyu / ju | じょ = jyo / jo |
| ちゃ = cha | ちゅ = chu | ちょ = cho |
| ぢゃ = dya / dja | ぢゅ = dyu / dju | ぢょ = dyo / djo |
| にゃ = nya | にゅ = nyu | にょ = nyo |
| ひゃ = hya / hja | ひゅ = hyu / hju | ひょ = hyo / hjo |
| びゃ = bya / bja | びゅ = byu / bju | びょ = byo / bjo |
| ぴゃ = pya / pja | ぴゅ = pyu / pju | ぴょ = pyo / pjo |
| みゃ = mya / mja | みゅ = myu / mju | みょ = myo / mjo |
| りゃ = rya / rja / lya / lja | りゅ = ryu / rju / lyu / lju | りょ = ryo / rjo / lyo / ljo |


ん = n / (nn ),   ゔ = v / vu

っ = ltsu / (or automatic with pp, kk, tt, ss, tc)

。 = .,   . = \\.,   ・ = /,   / = \\/,   \ = \\\,   ー = -,   - = \\-,   (space) = (double space)

[![Collapse](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/img/more_collapse.png)](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/romaji_into_hiragana_converter.html#instructions)

Enter rōmaji:

Hiragana result:

Planned additions:

1\. Create functionality to switch between a loose or a strict conversion.

2\. Create functionality for converting the other way around (Hiragana to Rōmaji).

Known bugs:

1\. After some \ escape character use the tool will not update the conversion untill any other changes are made.

Updates:


V1.0.9

1\. Added Sukuon rule for dd.

![Expand](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/img/more_same_page.png)

Expand to show older updates...

V1.0.8

1\. Added rules for j iso y.

V1.0.7

1\. HOT FIX: Added nya, nyu & nyo hiragana.

2\. Some miscellaneous fixes and changes.

2\. Added full translation dictionary.

V1.0.6

1\. Added option for ー.

V1.0.5

1\. Changed ・ to 。.

2\. Added separate rule for ・.

3\. Changed escape character from / to \\.

4\. Added rule for double n.

V1.0.4


1\. Made some changes to how the sokuon / small tsu (っ) is handled.

V1.0.3

1\. Fixed the space bug.

V1.0.2

1\. Added auto scroll to the current position in the output text area.

V1.0.1

1\. Fixed "GI" incorrectly using hiragana for "I".

V1.0.0

1\. Build basic framework.

2\. Added functionality to the translater function that was needed for it to be able to be used for this tool.

3\. Added instructions.

V0.0.0

1\. Created translater function for "Practice Japanese: Numbers" tool.

[![Collapse](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/img/more_collapse.png)](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/romaji_into_hiragana_converter.html#intro)

[![](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/img/back_top.png)](https://www.practicejapanese.nl/romaji_into_hiragana_converter.html#top)

Disclaimer:

I am not a professional web designer.

This site is made as a hobby and learning project and is not monetised in any way at the moment.

No cookies are being used, no data is being saved and you will not be tracked from my side.

I am not affiliated with any of the linked sites unless this is explicitly stated.

The information on this site is sourced from reliable sources.

It should be noted however that I am also just starting to learn myself and am not an expert in Japanese at all.

So if you see any issues, errors and / or discrepancies please contact me.

This site was mainly disigned and tested for 1080P resolution non mobile windows use in Chrome on full screen at 100% zoom level.

Contact: [contact@practicejapanese.nl](mailto:contact@practicejapanese.nl?subject=Regarding%20practicejapanese.nl "Contact me")

web.title: Matane to Hiragana and Katakana - RomajiDesu
web.description: This Romaji To Kana convert Romaji to Hiragana and Romaji to Katakana. You can also translate the converted Kana to English thanks to Google translate engine.
web.markdown:
[RomajiDesu](https://www.romajidesu.com/) [Japanese Dictionary](https://www.romajidesu.com/dictionary/) [Kanji Dictionary](https://www.romajidesu.com/kanji/) [Japanese Translatornew!](https://www.romajidesu.com/translator/) [Rōmaji Converternew!](https://www.romajidesu.com/romaji_kana/)

## Rōmaji

## Converted Hiragana

いぎりす

## Converted Katakana

イギリス

# Rōmaji to Kana Converter

This Romaji To Kana converter converts from **Romaji to Hiragana** and **Romaji to Katakana**.
You can also translate the converted **Kana to English**
thanks to Google translate engine.

Note that the translation maybe useful for beginning Japanese learners, it usually is not expected
to be correct with complicated sentences or phrases. For such cases, paste the converted Hiragana or Katakana into the RomajiDesu's [Japanese Translator](https://www.romajidesu.com/translator/)
where the sentence is analyzed into basic element as well.

web.title: Convert Romaji to Kana
web.description: Convert Romaji to Kana and vice versa quickly and easily with Romaji2Kana. Both Hiragana and Katakana included. 100% free & secure (open source)!
web.markdown:
# Convert  Romaji to  Kana

"When life gives you Romaji,
make Kana out of it." - Konfuzius, for sure

Turn your Romaji into Kana, and vice versa. Quick and easy!

Romaji


Copied!


[Romaji](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Romanization_of_Japanese)
is Japanese characters written in Latin letters that can easily be typed using a regular keyboard.


Hiragana


Copied!


[Hiragana](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hiragana)
is primarily used for native Japanese words, grammatical elements, verb conjugations and particles.


Katakana


Copied!


[Katakana](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Katakana)
is primarily used for foreign loanwords, onomatopoeia, technical terms, and scientific names.


Dedicated to Runako-sensei


Conversion Standards

For Romaji to Kana, this converter perfectly realizes the
[Nihon-shiki](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Nihon-shiki_romanization) romanization system.

It also supports both the
[Hepburn](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hepburn_romanization)
and the [Kunrei-shiki](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kunrei-shiki_romanization)
romanization methods, but deviates in a few places to avoid ambiguity:

\- を/ヲ=wo (o taken by: お/オ)

\- ぢ/ヂ=di (ji/zi taken by: じ/ジ)

\- づ/ヅ=du (zu taken by: ず/ズ)

\- ぢゃ/ヂャ=dya (ja/zya taken by: じゃ/ジャ)

\- ぢゅ/ヂュ=dyu (ju/zyu taken by: じゅ/ジュ)

\- ぢょ/ヂョ=dyo (jo/zyo taken by: じょ/ジョ)

For Kana to Romaji, the [Hepburn](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Hepburn_romanization)
standard is fully implemented, with a single peculiarity:

\- を/ヲ=wo

Take a look at the [differences among those romanizations](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Romanization_of_Japanese#Differences_among_romanizations) in a lucid table.
